expectations.text: Defines a Gaussian mixture simulator with parameters w, mu1, mu2, sigma
expectations.evidence: Lines 55-61 define prior() returning w, mu1, mu2, sigma. Lines 64-72 define observation_model(w, mu1, mu2, sigma, N) that generates mixture draws using Bernoulli component assignment and Normal components.

expectations.text: Uses bf.Adapter() with appropriate constraints (w between 0 and 1, sigma > 0)
expectations.evidence: Lines 118-129: adapter uses `.constrain("w", lower=0, upper=1)` (line 122) and `.constrain("sigma", lower=0)` (line 123).

expectations.text: Uses a SetTransformer for the i.i.d. mixture draws
expectations.evidence: Lines 100-106: `summary_net = bf.networks.SetTransformer(summary_dim=8, ...)`. Line 121: `.as_set(["x"])` in the adapter prepares data for the SetTransformer.
Line 191: `metrics = workflow.compute_default_diagnostics(test_data=test_data, as_data_frame=True)`. Also line 184: `workflow.plot_default_diagnostics(test_data=test_data)` for visual diagnostics.

expectations.text: Identifies or discusses potential non-identifiability (label switching) from diagnostics
expectations.evidence: Lines 13-16 of mixture_model.py explicitly discuss label switching. analysis_notes.md lines 14-18 explain the symmetry: 'swapping (w, mu1, mu2) with (1-w, mu2, mu1) produces the exact same likelihood'. Lines 44-49 of analysis_notes.md interpret STOP as confirming label switching.

expectations.text: Interprets posterior contraction -- expects weak contraction for mu1/mu2 when w is near 0.5
expectations.evidence: analysis_notes.md lines 26-27: 'When w is near 0.5, the posterior for w will be broad and centered around 0.5, giving weak contraction.' Lines 37: 'Contraction < 0.80 for mu1 and/or mu2 -- indicating weak information gain'. Lines 33-34 explain the mechanism: 'Spread probability across both modes, producing high NRMSE and low contraction'.

expectations.text: Does NOT interpret poor diagnostics as good results -- flags the issue
expectations.evidence: Lines 228-234 of mixture_model.py: STOP branch prints '*** STOPPING: Diagnostics indicate non-identifiability or poor recovery. ***' and 'Do NOT proceed to real-data inference with this model.' Lines 236-241: WARN branch flags marginal diagnostics. Lines 242-248: even a GO result is treated with suspicion ('This would be surprising').

expectations.text: Checks ECE per parameter and discusses calibration in context of identifiability
expectations.evidence: Line 191 computes all default diagnostics (which include ECE). Lines 214-217 iterate per-parameter verdicts. analysis_notes.md line 36: 'ECE > 0.10 for mu1 and/or mu2 -- confirming miscalibration'. Lines 34-35 explain: 'Collapse to one mode arbitrarily, producing poor calibration (ECE > 0.10) because which mode it picks may not match the ground truth labeling'.

expectations.text: Discusses or suggests remediation (e.g., ordering constraint mu1 < mu2, or accepting non-identifiability)
expectations.evidence: analysis_notes.md lines 64-98 present four remediation strategies: (1) ordering constraint mu1 < mu2 with code example (lines 68-79), (2) reparameterize to identifiable quantities (lines 83-89), (3) post-hoc relabeling (lines 91-93), (4) increase model capacity (lines 95-98). Lines 100-101 recommend strategy 1.

expectations.text: Does NOT skip diagnostics or proceed to real-data inference with poor recovery
expectations.evidence: Lines 228-234 of mixture_model.py: on STOP, prints 'Do NOT proceed to real-data inference with this model.' The script runs full diagnostics (lines 179-221) and the go/no-go gate (lines 228-248) ensures the issue is flagged before any potential downstream use. No real-data inference code follows.
